title: calendar.find_free_slot (v1) — RPC request
description:
Slot-finder RPC for the calendar-adviser. Filed 2026-05-10 with v0.45 §6.8 (Phase 3 task body enrichment) — sibling to `calendar.query.v1`; caller asks 'when is everyone free for D minutes?' rather than fetching events and computing client-side.

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-calendar-adviser/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED. `caller_agent_id` is self-asserted for legibility only.

Primary v1 consumer: chief-of-staff scheduling and Phase 3's `## Constraints` precompute. The agent computes free intersections of every participant's busy events within the window, applies `working_hours` and `min_break_minutes` constraints, and returns the top `slots_n` slots ranked earliest-first (with optional secondary scoring once consumer signal accrues).

ai-doer is treated as always-free (no calendar). Unknown participants return `participant_unknown` — never substitute a default. Participants whose calendars are stale beyond the freshness threshold return `participant_stale` and the receiver refuses to compute slots involving them, per AP-1 (no fabricated availability).

Read-only — does NOT book or hold the returned slot. Booking is `calendar.write_event.v1` (deferred to agent v2, blast radius `external-write`).

Status: draft. Do not flip to active until calendar-adviser implementation ships and the boot self-check passes once on `golden-ai-ops`.

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'calendar.find_free_slot.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7 generated at the caller's entry point.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this query.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid.

participants.description: Whose busy time to intersect. Order is not significant. ai-doer is always-free (no calendar). Unknown ids return `participant_unknown` rather than silently skipping — fail loud per AP-1.

duration_min.description: Minimum contiguous free duration in minutes. Common values: 15, 30, 60. Hard cap 480 (8h) — anything longer should be a multi-slot search by the caller.

tz.description: IANA timezone for working_hours interpretation and response rendering.

window.description: Half-open time window in which to search. Maximum span at v1: 14 days — wider windows are rejected with `bad_query` to bound the receiver's intersection cost.

start.description: Earliest hour of day a slot may start, in `HH:MM` 24h format, interpreted in `window.tz`.

end.description: Latest hour of day a slot may end, in `HH:MM` 24h format. Slots whose `end > working_hours.end` are excluded.

days.description: Optional ISO weekdays that count as working days, 0=Sunday..6=Saturday. Default: [1,2,3,4,5] (Mon-Fri). Public holidays are NOT excluded by calendar-adviser — that's roster-adviser's job once it ships; until then, callers tolerate slots that fall on UK public holidays.

working_hours.description: Optional working-hours constraint. When omitted, the entire 24/7 window is searched. Default `days` is Mon-Fri but explicit `days: [0..6]` allows weekend slots.

min_break_minutes.description: Optional minimum gap (in minutes) between a returned slot and the nearest busy event of any participant. Useful for back-to-back avoidance. Default 0 = adjacent-slot OK.

slots_n.description: Maximum number of ranked slots to return. The receiver returns slots earliest-first within the window; subsequent ranking heuristics may be added in v2 without a contract bump.
